what_it_does: A standalone deterministic test script that exercises the runtime.governance module: it checks action posture classification (AUTO vs CONFIRM), verifies that locked categories stay CONFIRM, runs an AUTO guard, confirms that a CONFIRM guard raises without confirmation while surfacing a decision to an Inbox, confirms that a confirmed CONFIRM guard runs, and checks that an inbox decision can be resolved.
observations: Uses a hand-rolled check() helper that accumulates pass/fail state into a global ok variable
Imports shutil but never uses it
Adds the project root to sys.path so it can import runtime.governance and store.fs_store
Creates a temporary directory for an FsStore-backed Inbox but does not clean it up
Tests policy classes: run/inspect/configure -> AUTO; code_build/destructive/external -> CONFIRM; unknown -> CONFIRM
Asserts that source_data, external, and frozen_contract are in gov.LOCKED and therefore locked to CONFIRM
Uses lambda expressions as the guarded action
Catches only gov.GovernanceError when testing the unconfirmed CONFIRM failure path
standouts: Encodes a deterministic AUTO/CONFIRM governance matrix directly in assertions
Tests the surfaced-decision inbox lifecycle: raise -> surface -> resolve
Hardcodes the locked-to-CONFIRM set as a safety contract
conventions: Docstring includes a direct shell run command
Manual test harness instead of pytest/unittest
sys.path.insert(0, parent_dir) for local package imports
concerns: Temporary directory is created but never removed
Global mutable ok state makes the test order-dependent and harder to parallelize
Catching only GovernanceError means other exceptions will escape without updating ok
The test assumes the first item in inbox.list() is the surfaced decision without verifying ordering guarantees
notes: A policy-as-code smoke test that pins low-risk AUTO behavior and high-risk CONFIRM/surfacing behavior for the governance layer.
questions: What do S7, D4, and D7 refer to in this codebase?
How is the Inbox intended to be presented to a human operator?
Is confirmation meant to be interactive, programmatic, or both?
What schema does FsStore.get_surfaced return beyond the resolved field?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to test the E6 act-unwatched policy and surfaced-decision inbox; the file does exactly that.
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to enforce an 'act-unwatched' rule: routine, cheap, reversible, internal actions may execute automatically, while any code-building, destructive, external, source-data, or frozen-contract action must stop, surface a decision, and wait for explicit approval.
novelty: It couples a simple posture classifier with a persisted surfaced-decision inbox in one executable, deterministic test rather than separating policy rules from audit/escalation tests.
contribution: An executable contract that captures which action classes are AUTO, which are CONFIRM, which are permanently locked, and how unconfirmed actions are surfaced and later resolved — useful as a regression guard for governance behavior.
summary_for_embedding: A deterministic manual test that validates an AUTO/CONFIRM governance posture and a surfaced-decision inbox using runtime.governance and an FsStore-backed Inbox.
intention_for_embedding: To specify and verify that low-risk actions run unwatched while risky or locked classes require confirmation and surface decisions for resolution.

symbols.does: A manual assertion helper that updates a global ok flag and prints a PASS/FAIL line.
symbols.description: check(label, cond) evaluates a boolean condition, ANDs it into the module-level ok variable, and prints a formatted pass/fail message. It is the test harness's minimal assertion primitive.

symbols.does: Runs the E6 governance test sequence and returns 0 on full success or 1 on any failure.
symbols.description: main() executes the governance policy checks: posture classification, locked categories, AUTO guard execution, CONFIRM guard rejection and surfacing, confirmed CONFIRM execution, and inbox resolution. It prints a final pass/fail banner based on the accumulated ok flag.
